commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7b36dfcf3ee9e3c9bbcdd5bf7a25c73c6565d9eb)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Telegram provider proof metadata, a post-send outbound validation hook, Telegram topic proof validation, and regression tests for cron Telegram topic delivery.

PR surface: Source +412, Tests +654. Total +1066 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: source inspection shows the PR exports a new plugin-facing validation hook and invokes it after send results are available. The original live Telegram topic behavior is not reproduced because the PR has no live Telegram artifact.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer API/compatibility judgment and contributor live Telegram proof; the remaining blockers are not a safe narrow automation repair.

target.title: [codex] Verify Telegram topic proof for cron delivery
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where cron jobs that deliver to a configured non-General Telegram forum topic could be marked delivered without Telegram proving the actual provider topic. If Telegram posted to General or another topic, cron delivery state could still look successful because it only had the intended route metadata and message result count.

## Why This Change Was Made

Telegram sends now surface provider-confirmed `chatId` / `messageThreadId` proof in `meta.telegram.messages[]`.

Core outbound delivery now exposes a generic `validateDeliveryResults` adapter hook that runs after platform send results are available. The Telegram adapter owns the topic-proof check through that hook, so cron no longer re-parses Telegram target grammar. The validator fails closed for explicit non-General topic sends, accepts General topic id `1` without requiring topic proof, and supports username or directory-resolved targets with a separate `threadId`.

## User Impact

Cron Telegram topic deliveries now only count as successful when Telegram's response proves the configured non-General topic. Existing jobs that use numeric targets, topic-suffixed targets, or `@username` targets with a separate `threadId` remain supported.

## Evidence

Current head: `7b36dfcf3e fix cron telegram topic delivery proof`

Local validation:

- `node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check` on the 9 changed files: passed
- `git diff --check`: passed
- `node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts`: 4 files passed, 360 tests passed
- `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental false --pretty false`: passed
- `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --declaration false --incremental false --pretty false`: passed
- `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --noEmit --declaration false --incremental false --pretty false`: passed

Live behavior proof, redacted:

- Ran current-branch `sendMessageTelegram(...)` against a private Telegram forum topic with expected `messageThreadId=9983`.
- Telegram returned the same provider `messageThreadId` in `meta.telegram.messages[]`; chat id and message id are redacted.

```json
{
  "sentAt": "2026-06-28T05:30:07.754Z",
  "expectedMessageThreadId": 9983,
  "result": {
    "messageId": "msg...0432",
    "chatId": "-100...0061",
    "meta": {
      "telegram": {
        "chatId": "-100...0061",
        "messageThreadId": 9983,
        "requestedMessageThreadId": 9983,
        "messages": [
          {
            "messageId": "msg...0432",
            "chatId": "-100...0061",
            "messageThreadId": 9983,
            "requestedMessageThreadId": 9983
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Supplemental Mantis run:

- Mantis Telegram Desktop Proof passed on the previous head: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28311537748
- The uploaded evidence reported no desktop before/after GIF because this PR changes cron/Telegram delivery proof validation rather than Telegram Desktop rendering.
